setting.note: Ishmael signs aboard the whaler Pequod out of Nantucket and is swept into Captain Ahab's monomaniac hunt for the white whale that took his leg, a voyage across the world's oceans toward annihilation. Melville roots the epic in the real whaling world of New England: the port of New Bedford where Ishmael meets Queequeg, the island of Nantucket where the Pequod sails, and the far Pacific grounds where the White Whale at last is found.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Herman Melville (d. 1891), first published 1851. Mapped as a cited work: we store no text and reproduce no passages here. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. This entry reproduces no text; the public-domain original is widely available.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. The original text is in the public domain.
rights.attribution: Moby-Dick, Herman Melville (1851). Locations mapped by Read the Places.

palette.note: The deep blue of the sea that carries the Pequod to the ends of the earth, indifferent and bottomless beneath Ahab's rage. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
